name: Gastroenteritis
notes: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines; often acute and associated with dietary indiscretion or infection.

name: Pancreatitis
notes: Inflammation of the pancreas; can range from mild to life-threatening and often causes significant abdominal discomfort.

name: Inflammatory Bowel Disease
notes: Chronic inflammation of the intestines, typically causing long-term digestive signs and weight loss.

name: Intestinal Parasites
notes: Parasitic infections such as roundworms, hookworms, or whip-worms affecting the intestinal tract.

name: Gastric Dilatation-Volvulus
notes: Also known as bloat; a life-threatening emergency that requires immediate veterinary attention.

name: Foreign Body Obstruction
notes: Ingestion of an object that becomes lodged in the gastrointestinal tract, potentially requiring surgery.

name: Chronic Hepatitis
notes: Long-term inflammation of the liver that can progress to liver failure if untreated.

name: Cholestasis / Bile Duct Obstruction
notes: Reduced or blocked bile flow from the liver; may be caused by stones, inflammation, or tumors.

name: Portosystemic Shunt
notes: Abnormal blood vessel shunts blood around the liver, reducing detoxification and causing neurologic signs.

name: Liver Neoplasia
notes: Primary or metastatic tumors affecting the liver; signs are often chronic and nonspecific.

name: Hypothyroidism
notes: Common in middle-aged dogs. Often presents with chronic low energy and gradual weight gain despite no change in diet.

name: Diabetes Mellitus
notes: Characterized by persistent high blood sugar. Classic pattern is increased thirst, increased urination, and weight loss.

name: Cushing's Disease (Hyperadrenocorticism)
notes: Caused by chronic excess cortisol. Often presents with a pot-bellied abdomen, panting, and increased thirst/urination.

name: Addison's Disease (Hypoadrenocorticism)
notes: Often has vague, intermittent signs like GI upset and low energy that can suddenly progress to collapse during an Addisonian crisis.

name: Obesity
notes: Chronic condition that increases risk of diabetes, joint disease, and cardiovascular stress.
